# AI Registry / KI Register

> AI Registry (English) / KI Register (German) is a use-case-first AI governance platform for organisations required to comply with the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689). It provides structured documentation, risk classification, governance workflows, and audit-ready evidence management for AI systems in use.

For the complete platform reference in a single file, see [Full Reference](https://airegist.com/llms-full.txt).

## What AI Registry Does

AI Registry is the operational layer between an organisation's AI usage and its legal obligations under the EU AI Act. Organisations use it to:

- **Register AI use cases** with structured metadata (purpose, data categories, affected groups, decision influence)
- **Classify risk** per the EU AI Act four-tier model (minimal, limited, high-risk, prohibited)
- **Run guided risk assessments** aligned to EU AI Act Annex III high-risk categories
- **Manage governance** with human oversight documentation, review cycles, ISO 42001 / ISO 27001 alignment
- **Generate AI policies** at three levels: Commitment (1 page), Framework (3–5 pages), Enterprise (8–10 pages)
- **Export audit dossiers** as PDF or structured data for regulatory evidence
- **Operate a Trust Portal** — a public governance proof page for external stakeholders, auditors, and partners
- **Assess supply chain** — verify AI governance requirements in the vendor/supplier chain
- **Accept external submissions** via access codes, allowing team members or business partners to submit AI use cases without a login

## Core Concepts

- **Use Case / Einsatzfall** — the primary unit. Every AI system in use is registered as a use case with fields for purpose, risk class, data categories, usage context, decision influence, responsible party, and oversight model.
- **Risk Classes** — Minimal Risk, Limited Risk (transparency obligations), High-Risk (Annex III), Prohibited (unacceptable risk). The platform maps to official EU AI Act English terminology.
- **Deployer / Provider** — EU AI Act roles. Most organisations are deployers. The platform is built for deployer obligations (Art. 26 ff. EU AI Act).
- **Human-in-the-loop (HITL)** — oversight model option required for high-risk systems. The platform tracks and documents this.
- **Register** — an organisation's complete inventory of AI use cases with their governance status.
- **Governance Control Centre** — the premium tier that adds policy engine, audit export, trust portal, batch operations, and portfolio analytics.

## Platform Architecture

- Web app at https://airegist.com
- Authentication: email/password with Firebase Auth
- Data: Firestore (EU region)
- All user data stored in EU (GDPR Art. 26 joint controllers: ZukunftBilden GmbH + BewusstseinBilden UG)
- Languages: German (default, /de/...) and English (/en/...) with automatic browser detection
- Access: SaaS subscription (Free Register tier + Governance Control Centre Pro/Enterprise)

## Pages and Features

- [Home / Landing](https://airegist.com/en): Product overview, sign-up and invitation code entry
- [My Register](https://airegist.com/en/my-register): The main dashboard — list and manage all registered AI use cases
- [EU AI Act Viewer](https://airegist.com/en/law): Full text of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 with recitals, articles and annexes, searchable
- [Intake / External Submission](https://airegist.com/en/intake): Public form for submitting AI use cases via access code — no login required
- [Governance Control Centre](https://airegist.com/en/control): Premium governance dashboard — reviews, policies, exports, portfolio, trust
- [Settings](https://airegist.com/en/settings): Organisation governance settings, RACI matrix, incident process, AI policy configuration
- [Trust Portal](https://airegist.com/en/trust): Public governance evidence page per organisation (accessible without login)
- [Verification](https://airegist.com/en/verify): Verify AI Registry quality seals and certifications by code

## EU AI Act Compliance Scope

The platform supports deployer obligations under Regulation (EU) 2024/1689:

- Art. 9: Risk management system documentation for high-risk AI
- Art. 13: Transparency and provision of information
- Art. 14: Human oversight — model selection and documentation
- Art. 26: Deployer obligations including use-case registration and risk assessment
- Art. 29: Obligations when deploying general-purpose AI
- Annex III: High-risk AI system categories (biometrics, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, justice, critical infrastructure)
- Recital 47 / Art. 6: Risk classification methodology

## Industries Served

AI Registry is used across sectors with significant EU AI Act exposure:

**Healthcare & Life Sciences** — AI-assisted diagnostics, patient risk scoring, clinical decision support. High-risk under Annex III Art. 6(c). Requires risk assessment, human oversight, and evidence documentation.

**HR & Recruitment** — CV screening, candidate ranking, performance evaluation AI. High-risk under Annex III Art. 6(d). Particularly scrutinised under EU AI Act and GDPR Art. 22.

**Finance & Banking** — Credit scoring, fraud detection, algorithmic trading signals, AML systems. High-risk or limited-risk depending on automation level. Requires transparency obligations.

**Education & Training** — Student assessment AI, learning path personalisation, automated grading. High-risk under Annex III Art. 6(b) when used in educational institutions.

**Public Sector & Government** — Benefits eligibility, permit processing, law enforcement analytics. Highest scrutiny under EU AI Act. Some use cases near prohibited category.

**Legal & Consulting** — Contract analysis, legal research assistants, compliance automation. Typically limited risk with transparency obligations.

**Manufacturing & Operations** — Predictive maintenance, quality control vision AI, supply chain optimisation. Generally minimal to limited risk unless safety-critical.

**Professional Services** — Client-facing AI tools, report generation, data analysis. Typically minimal risk but documentation required for accountability.

## Agent Kit & API

AI Registry provides a programmatic API for enterprise integrations and agent workflows:

- REST API for reading and writing use case data to the register
- Agent Kit: structured access for AI agents to query governance status, risk classifications, and compliance exposure
- Access via Settings → Agent Kit & API (requires authentication)
- Use cases: automated use-case registration pipelines, compliance monitoring agents, ERP/HR system integrations

## Pricing Tiers

- **Free Register** — Unlimited use case registration, basic risk classification, intake access codes
- **Governance Control Centre (Pro)** — Policy generator, audit export, trust portal, ISO alignment, review workflows, portfolio analytics
- **Enterprise** — Multi-organisation structure, advanced reporting, custom integrations, dedicated support

## Legal

- Privacy Policy: https://airegist.com/en/privacy
- Legal Notice: https://airegist.com/en/legal-notice
- Terms and Conditions: https://airegist.com/en/terms
- Governing law: Austrian law (Landesgericht Salzburg)
- Data controller: ZukunftBilden GmbH + BewusstseinBilden UG (haftungsbeschränkt), joint controllers per GDPR Art. 26
- EU ODR platform: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr
